column2: Rarely (a few times a month or less)

column10: My favorite feature was the time-series charts for indicators like CO₂ emissions and GDP. They make it very easy to see trends over time and quickly understand how the indicators change across years.
column11: Overall the dashboard was easy to use, but on mobile screens some charts require a bit of scrolling and zooming to view comfortably. Improving the mobile layout could make the experience smoother.
column12: I expected there might be a comparison feature that allows users to view multiple countries or regions side-by-side. That could make it easier to analyze differences across regions.
column13: I would prioritize adding comparison functionality between countries or regions, as it would make the dashboard more useful for exploring and interpreting the data.

column10: My favorite feature is the Energy vs Valence scatter plot. I think that this chart is the most useful because it combines multiple variables (energy, valence, genre, popularity) and supports interactive filtering, making it the best tool for discovering patterns in the music dataset.
column11:
The most confusing part of the experience was the relationship between filtering and selection in the dashboard.

While the dashboard shows counters like “Total,” “Filtered,” and “Selected” tracks, it is not immediately clear how these states differ or how actions affect them. For example, when using the brush tool on the Energy vs Valence scatter plot, it selects a subset of tracks, but it may not be obvious whether this is applying a filter or simply highlighting data temporarily.
column12: Yes. One feature I expected to see but could not find was the ability to play or preview the songs directly from the dashboard. Since the dashboard shows detailed information about tracks (title, artist, genre, and audio features), it would be very helpful if users could listen to a short preview or open the song in Spotify. This would make the exploration more engaging and help users connect the audio features with how the music actually sounds.
column13: If only one improvement could be prioritized, it should be making the interaction between filtering and selection clearer and more intuitive. For example, adding a short instruction or guide.
